Digital Archive // v2.0

Return to Archive Code // CASE STUDY

ATLAS

A real-time visualization system that reveals how large codebases are structured. Watching a codebase and its connections emerge as a living graph helps me understand the "gravity" of a project's architecture rather than just reading lines of code.

GitHub

01 — ARCHIVE ENTRY

ATLAS analyzes a codebase incrementally and renders it as a 3D force-directed graph. Each file is a node; dependencies are edges. You watch the structure emerge as ATLAS reads the project.

02 — INTENTION // RESOLUTION

The Friction

Reading a large unfamiliar codebase is slow. You either read files one by one or look at static diagrams that may not reflect reality. Neither gives you a live, spatial sense of organization.

The Solution

ATLAS reads the project directory and assembles the graph in real-time. Node size is determined by PageRank centrality, reflecting architectural influence rather than just file size.

03 — TECH STACK

JavaScript d3-force Node.js PageRank WebSockets Three.js

FINAL REFLECTIONS

Implementing PageRank from scratch was highly educational. I confirmed that watching structure emerge is more informative than seeing it all at once.

Have an idea?
Let's explore it.

Chill Contact
